active_goal: Improve MemoryOS Lite v3 into a benchmark-usable Letta-style agent memory system for LongMemEval and LoCoMo, without demo-only phase completion, without hiding case-level regressions, and without enabling the v3 kernel by default.

context_bundle.summary: Phase 14 should audit the opt-in kernel memory-action loop, keep archive_write as the baseline unless RED evidence shows broader tools are needed, and add explicit post-action verification without changing defaults. Parallel benchmark gates must use isolated DATA_DIR values.

objective: Define the smallest credible Letta-style kernel memory-action loop audit that proves durable write, replay integrity, and post-action verification without enabling the v3 kernel by default.
non_goals: broadening the kernel beyond the minimum supported action set without RED evidence
claiming benchmark improvement from trace-only changes
failing_tests_required_before_implementa: Add a kernel test that currently fails because successful archive_write does not yet emit an explicit post-action verification trace.
Add a kernel test that currently fails because replay-tampered approval handling does not yet prove zero verification side effects.
If the plan expands supported tools, add explicit denials for unsupported memory tool names before enabling them.
usable_ack_checklist: Kernel path remains opt-in and v3 default remains unchanged.
Approved memory action writes to the real store and surfaces through the real v3/context path.
Replay tampering and unsupported tools are denied with no side effects.
Verification traces are durable and visible in kernel_trace_events when the kernel is enabled.
No benchmark improvement claim is made from trace-only evidence.
review_focus: v1 fallback and kernel opt-in preservation
